h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the checklist was the evidence, or at least enough for sign-off
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was simply following instructions to perform the physical test in order to satisfy the administrative requirements for sign-off.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Start with the unsafe condition: what latch behavior would stop the trial, even with every box ticked?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts the paradigm from administrative checklist verification to physical risk assessment in response to the learner's confusion about what constitutes valid evidence.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: For this trial, use this working threshold: if the contact point moves, twists, drops, or becomes unpredictable under load, treat support as changed.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly addresses the learner's stated uncertainty by providing a specific, observable threshold to resolve the ambiguity of the concept in real time.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I thought the checklist was the evidence, or at least enough for sign-off

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: For this trial, use this working threshold: if the contact point moves, twists, drops, or becomes unpredictable under load, treat support as changed.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly addresses the learner's stated uncertainty by providing a specific, observable threshold to resolve the ambiguity of the concept in real time.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Start with the unsafe condition: what latch behavior would stop the trial, even with every box ticked?
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts the paradigm from administrative checklist verification to physical risk assessment in response to the learner's confusion about what constitutes valid evidence.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Start with the unsafe condition: what latch behavior would stop the trial, even with every box ticked?
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts the paradigm from administrative checklist verification to physical risk assessment in response to the learner's confusion about what constitutes valid evidence.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue trying to justify the doubled side or argue that four boxes somehow equates to double area.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor breaks the conflated concept into two distinct, testable claims to resolve the learner's cognitive mismatch.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: This corner-to-corner line is left on the sheet. Build the square on that line.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor introduces a completely new geometric object to bypass the learner's impasse and adapt to their revised framing that the doubled side must be abandoned.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: This corner-to-corner line is left on the sheet. Build the square on that line.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor introduces a completely new geometric object to bypass the learner's impasse and adapt to their revised framing that the doubled side must be abandoned.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor breaks the conflated concept into two distinct, testable claims to resolve the learner's cognitive mismatch.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor breaks the conflated concept into two distinct, testable claims to resolve the learner's cognitive mismatch.
